source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.



Our business is growing and so is our sales team! We're hiring Business Development Representatives to engage with prospects and build relationships.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Our BDRs drive top-of-funnel activity by engaging prospects and generating high-quality leads to fuel SafeLease’s sales pipeline.



To drive our mission forward, this role will:

 - Forge strong connections: Act as the first point of contact, building relationships with future partners through compelling outreach and communication

 - Collaborate dynamically: Partner with Account Executives to qualify leads and align on strategies that maximize pipeline impact

 - Embrace growth opportunities: Join a sales team where ambition and performance pave the way for career advancement

 - Drive measurable impact: Contribute to a fast-growing company where exceptional results are rewarded with exceptional recognition and compensation




ABOUT YOU

You are adept at communicating with commercial real estate professionals and managing early sales activities. Ideal candidates are energized by learning about new and existing products, are coachable with the ability to work autonomously, and want to be a part of a winning team.



You’ll bring these skills and strengths:

 - 1+ years in a similar role (optional) with a strong desire to build a thriving sales career (required)

 - A performance-driven mindset--you're motivated by a compensation structure that rewards top performance

 - Outgoing and comfortable interacting with large numbers of prospects on a daily basis

 - Exceptional communication skills with an above-average ability to articulate ideas clearly and persuasively across multiple channels

 - Ability to thrive in dynamic environments, embracing challenges and the unknowns of a growing company

 - Collaborative, open to feedback, and eager to strategize with sales team members to achieve shared goals

 - Natural curiousity and a bias towards listening, you always seek to understand prospects’ needs

 - P&C insurance interest and/or experience a plus



Why SafeLease?

The tech: Our prospects convert fast because we’re solving real problems and delivering serious value to commercial real estate owners.

The team: We’re a team of seasoned pros and sharp operators who know how to move fast and build smart. High standards, low ego.

The stability: We’re well-funded, growing fast, and we make sure our team shares in that success with competitive pay and equity.

The employee experience: We also offer unlimited PTO, full health benefits, flexible work setups, and the kind of culture where people want to show up to do their best work.



If you don't have all the qualifications listed, don't worry! We understand everyone's career pa

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.



SafeLease is looking for a driven Full Stack Engineer. In this role, you’ll work across the entire stack building, testing, and shipping features. You'll collaborate closely with company leadership, playing a key role in solving high-impact technical challenges and shaping the future of our platform.




ABOUT THE ROLE




To drive our mission forward, this role will:

 - Contribute across the stack — Write clean, maintainable code across our platform with a focus on reliability, performance, and simplicity

 - Build customer-facing features — Collaborate with Product and Design to create intuitive, user-first functionality

 - Tackle technical challenges — Help debug issues, improve code quality, and strengthen system reliability

 - Focus on growth and learning — Take ownership of your work while being open to feedback and opportunities to grow as an engineer

 - Work collaboratively — Participate in code reviews, planning sessions, and team discussions to build shared understanding and alignment

 - Embrace iteration and improvement — Contribute ideas that help us move faster, build smarter, and work better as a team
   
   


ABOUT YOU

You're a curious and resourceful builder who enjoys solving real-world problems through code. You’re eager to grow your technical skills, collaborate with others, and contribute to a fast-paced, mission-driven team.



Your experience:

 - 2+ years of experience in full-stack software development with exposure to shipping real-world applications

 - Background in CS/EE, a coding bootcamp, or equivalent hands-on experience building production systems

 - Comfortable with Frontend (TypeScript, JavaScript, React), Backend (Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs), and Infrastructure (relational databases, Git, AWS) technologies

 - Willingness to learn and adopt best-in-class tools like Linear, Cursor, and other fast-moving AI-powered developer tools

 - Solid communication skills and a collaborative mindset—willing to ask questions, give input, and support your teammates

 - DevOps experience is a bonus, especially if you’re interested in understanding how code runs in production

 - Excited to work in-person or hybrid from our beautiful downtown Austin office
   
   

Why SafeLease?

The tech: Our prospects convert fast because we’re solving real problems and delivering serious value to commercial real estate owners.

The team: We’re a team of seasoned pros and sharp operators who know how to move fast and build smart. High standards, low ego.

The stability: We’re well-funded, growing fast, and we make sure our team shares in that success with competitive pay and equity.

The employee experience: We also offer unlimited PTO, full health benefits, flexible work setups

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.

SafeLease is looking for a driven Senior Full Stack Software Engineer. In this role, you’ll work across the entire stack building, testing, and shipping features. You'll collaborate closely with company leadership, playing a key role in solving high-impact technical challenges and shaping the future of our platform.




ABOUT THE ROLE




Joining our Engineering team at this stage means your work will have a huge influence on future developers, the codebase, and company culture. This role is based out of our downtown Austin office—full time at first and then hybrid after onboarding.



To drive our mission forward, this role will:

 - Lead development across the stack — Architect, build, and ship high-quality code across our entire platform, setting the standard for technical excellence

 - Design scalable, elegant solutions — Collaborate with Product to solve complex problems with simple, customer-centric features

 - Own issue resolution — Triage, prioritize, and resolve system level issues while guiding others on best practices for quality and reliability

 - Champion continuous improvement — Proactively identify opportunities to improve system performance, code health, and team efficiency

 - Advocate for the user — Build with empathy and a deep understanding of customer needs, ensuring every solution adds meaningful value

 - Mentor and elevate others — Share expertise, coach teammates, and help shape a culture of learning, growth, and technical rigor
   
   


ABOUT YOU

A collaborative and innovative thinker who has experience shipping code in a fast-paced, agile environment will make a huge impact at SafeLease!



Your experience:

 - 5+ years in a full-stack software development role with a track record of owning and delivering complex features or systems

 - Degree in CS/EE

 - Deep proficiency with Frontend (TypeScript, JavaScript, React), Backend (Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs), and Infrastructure (Relational databases, Git, AWS) systems and languages

 - Ability to learn and adapt to best-in-class tools including but not limited to Linear, Cursor, and other rapidly advancing AI tools

 - Strong collaboration and communication skills, with a proven ability to lead discussions, drive alignment, and mentor peers

 - DevOps-related experience is a plus—skills that go beyond just writing code and own how that code runs in production

 - Desire to work in-person or hybrid from our beautiful downtown Austin office
   
   

Why SafeLease?

The tech: Our prospects convert fast because we’re solving real problems and delivering serious value to commercial real estate owners.

The team: We’re a team of seasoned pros and sharp operators who know how to move fast and build smart. High standard

source_jobs.title: Agency Development Manager
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.




THE ROLE

As our Agency Development Manager, you will be the face of SafeLease to the brokerage community. You will be responsible for identifying, appointing, and managing relationships with independent agents as well as regional and national brokerages.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build the Network: Identify and recruit high-performing commercial lines agencies and brokers who specialize in our core and adjacent verticals (self-storage, light industrial, marinas, hangars, etc).

 - Onboard & Activate: Lead the appointment process. After signing producer agreements; you’ll train producers on how to use the SafeLease portal to quote and bind business in minutes and ensure they are successfully utilizing our platform and understand our appetite.

 - Relationship Management: Act as the primary point of contact for our producer partners. Conduct regular account reviews, provide appetite updates, and gather feedback to improve our product. Be hyper responsive to their needs and obsessed with making them successful working with SafeLease.

 - Product Advocacy: Educate producers on our insurance products and technology advantage — showing them how our data-driven underwriting and faster turnaround times leads to better pricing and faster commissions for them.

 - Drive Premium Growth: Own the entire pipeline from initial submission through to bind. You will partner closely with our underwriters to ensure high-quality risks are being prioritized.

 - Own Program Business: Build distribution/referral relationships with our existing property management customers and other relevant industry parties.
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - The Connector: You have strong commercial property insurance contacts and know how to navigate the hierarchy of a large brokerage.

 - The Industry Guru: You understand commercial P&C fundamentals but are bored by legacy systems. You’re excited to show brokers a better way to work.

 - The Strategist: You proactively identify the best opportunities & build a plan to capture them.

 - Experience: 5+ years in insurance distribution, agency management, or wholesale brokerage. Active surplus lines producer license is highly preferred (or relevant surplus lines experience.)
   
   


HOW WILL SUCCESS BE MEASURED?

To ensure this person is successful, you should measure them on:

 1. New Appointments: Number of high-quality agencies signed per month.

 2. Submission Flow: Volume of "Qualified Submissions" coming through the broker channel.

 3. Activation Rate: The percentage of appointed agencies that bind at least one policy within their first 60 days.

 4. Retention: Year-over-year renewal rate of the broker-produced book of business.
    
    

Why SafeLease?

The tech: Our prospects conve

source_jobs.title: Strategy & Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.



We are seeking a Strategy & Operations Manager with a consulting background to lead cross-departmental initiatives, partnering with Product, Go-To-Market (GTM), Support, and other teams to drive strategic and operational excellence. This role demands exceptional project management skills, strategic vision, and the ability to align cross-functional efforts with organizational goals in a fast-paced environment.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Oversee and drive high-impact, cross-functional projects, ensuring alignment with company strategy and timely execution.

 - Collaborate with Product, GTM, and Support teams to identify opportunities for operational improvements and implement scalable solutions.

 - Develop and monitor KPIs to evaluate the success of strategic initiatives and operational processes.

 - Lead the creation of project plans, timelines, and resource allocation to ensure successful project delivery.

 - Provide strategic recommendations to senior leadership based on data analysis and industry trends.

 - Facilitate cross-departmental collaboration, managing stakeholder relationships to drive consensus and outcomes.

 - Mentor and guide junior team members in project execution and operational strategy.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Business, Management, or a related field; MBA or advanced degree strongly preferred.

 - 2-8 years of experience in management consulting, with a focus on strategy, operations, or transformation projects.

 - Exceptional project management skills, with experience leading large-scale, cross-functional initiatives.

 - Strong analytical skills, with expertise in tools like Excel, SQL, or Tableau for strategic analysis and reporting.

 - Proven ability to influence and manage senior stakeholders across multiple departments.

 - Experience working with Product, GTM, or Support teams in dynamic, high-growth environments.

 - Ability to navigate complexity and drive results in ambiguous, fast-paced settings.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience leading operational or strategic initiatives in tech or startup environments.

 - Proficiency with project management tools (e.g., Asana, Jira, or Monday.com http://Monday.com).

 - Deep understanding of GTM strategies or product development processes.

 - Experience in the self-storage and/or insurance industry.

Why SafeLease?

The tech: Our prospects convert fast because we’re solving real problems and delivering serious value to commercial real estate owners.

The team: We’re a team of seasoned pros and sharp operators who know how to move fast and build smart. High standards, low ego.

The stability: We’re well-funded, growing fast, and we make sure our team shares in that success with competitive pay and e

source_jobs.title: Builder
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.




WHY "BUILDER"?

The best people on our team start with a business problem, figure out what should exist to solve it, and then build it, often end-to-end. They talk to stakeholders, sketch out solutions, make product and design decisions, write the code, and ship it. They use AI tools not as a novelty but as a core part of how they work. The line between "product thinking" and "engineering" isn't one they spend much time drawing.

We call this role Builder because that's what it actually is. You'll work in small pods with a GM who sets direction, but how you get there and what you build along the way is largely up to you.

If you're looking for a well-defined backlog and a narrow lane, this probably isn't the right fit. If you want the autonomy to solve real problems and the context to understand why they matter, keep reading.




ABOUT THE ROLE

To drive our mission forward, this role will:

 - Start with the problem, not the ticket. Understand the business context behind what you're building. Know why it matters to customers, to the business, and to the team.

 - Own outcomes, not just output. Take features from idea to production. That means participating in product and design decisions, not waiting for a spec to land in your lap.

 - Build across the full stack. Write clean, maintainable code across frontend, backend, and infrastructure with a focus on reliability, performance, and simplicity.

 - Use AI as a force multiplier. Leverage AI-powered tools to move faster, explore solutions more broadly, and spend your time on the problems that actually require human judgment.

 - Improve the system, not just your code. Identify opportunities to make the platform better, the team faster, and the product sharper. Bring ideas, not just implementations.

 - Collaborate with high context. Work closely with your pod, leadership, and cross-functional partners. Share what you know, ask what you don't.
   
   


ABOUT YOU

You're a problem solver who happens to write code, not the other way around. You care about what gets built as much as how it gets built. You're comfortable with ambiguity, energized by ownership, and sharp enough to develop a working understanding of the business you're building for.

Your experience:

 - 2-5+ years of experience in full-stack software development with a track record of shipping real-world applications

 - Background in CS/EE, a coding bootcamp, or equivalent hands-on experience building production systems

 - Proficiency across Frontend (TypeScript, JavaScript, React), Backend (Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs), and Infrastructure (relational databases, Git, AWS)

 - Demonstrated fluency with AI-powered development tools (Cursor, Claude, Copilot, etc.) — not just awar

source_jobs.title: Controller
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.




ABOUT THE ROLE

SafeLease is hiring a Controller to lead all accounting, financial reporting, and internal controls across the organization — with full ownership spanning our Managing General Agent (MGA) operations and our surplus lines insurance carrier. The role owns dual-track reporting: U.S. GAAP financial statements for SafeLease and its consolidated entities, and statutory financial statements filed with state insurance regulators under NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles. The ideal candidate combines a Big Four audit foundation with hands-on accounting experience inside an insurance carrier, and a track record of building and scaling finance functions in a high-growth environment. This is a high-visibility leadership role reporting to the CEO with direct exposure to senior leadership and strategic decision-making.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own and oversee all accounting operations across the MGA and the surplus lines carrier, including general ledger management, month-end / quarter-end / year-end close, and intercompany accounting between the producer and the underwriter.

 - Prepare and deliver timely, accurate financial statements in accordance with both U.S. GAAP and Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP), including the NAIC P&C Annual Statement, quarterly statements, and audited statutory financial statements.

 - Manage statutory reporting and regulatory filings: NAIC Schedules F (reinsurance), P (loss reserves), D (investments), and T (premium by state); Risk-Based Capital (RBC) calculations; IRIS ratios; and domiciliary and non-admitted / surplus lines state filings.

 - Oversee MGA accounting under the NAIC Managing General Agents Act (Model #225), including premium trust / fiduciary accounts, the MGA-to-carrier reporting cycle, and quarterly accounting packages submitted to the carrier in a form usable for completion of the Annual Statement.

 - Design, implement, and maintain robust internal controls and financial policies that scale with organizational growth (SOX-style frameworks preferred).

 - Lead the annual statutory and GAAP audits and serve as the primary liaison with external auditors, tax advisors, state insurance examiners, and the engaged actuary.

 - Build, mentor, and develop a high-performing accounting team; establish best-in-class processes and a culture of continuous improvement.

 - Manage consolidated financial reporting across SafeLease entities, including intercompany transactions and eliminations between the MGA and the carrier.

 - Partner with the CEO and senior leadership to provide financial insights, variance analysis, and strategic recommendations.

 - Own premium and claims accounting cycles, ceded and assumed reinsurance accounting, deferred

If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.



About the Role:

We are launching a new vertical and looking for a Business Development (BDR) Manager to build and lead our Manufactured Housing BDR team from the ground up. This is a true zero-to-one opportunity — you will own the playbook, define the outbound motion, and help establish SafeLease as the go-to insurance provider across a market of over 40,000 manufactured housing communities nationwide. You will be responsible for hiring, onboarding, developing, and managing a team of BDRs who prospect into community operators, owners, and management companies. For the right person, this is a rare chance to scale and optimize an untapped vertical with the full support of a high-growth, profitable company behind you.



What you will do:

 - Lead, coach, and develop a team of BDRs focused exclusively on the manufactured housing vertical.

 - Own team performance against weekly and monthly targets for meetings booked and pipeline created.

 - Conduct regular 1:1s, call reviews, and role-play sessions to improve prospecting and discovery skills.

 - Oversee and continuously refine the team's outbound strategy across cold calling and email

 - Hire and onboard new BDR team members and ramp them to productivity quickly.

 - Partner with Account Executives to ensure smooth handoffs and strong meeting-to-opportunity conversion.

 - Partner with marketing to align on ICP targeting, messaging, and campaign strategy for the MH segment.

 - Monitor and report on key BDR metrics — dials, connects, meetings booked, pipeline created — and present weekly updates to sales leadership.

 - Enforce CRM hygiene standards and consistent data entry practices across the team.

 - Contribute to playbook development for the manufactured housing vertical.
   
   

About you:

 - 1+ years of BDR/SDR experience in B2B SaaS, technology or insurance.

 - 2+ years of BDR manager with direct responsibility for managing and developing BDR/SDR performance.

 - Demonstrated track record of meeting or exceeding outbound pipeline targets.

 - Strong coaching instincts — you get energy from helping others improve, not just hitting your own number.

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-moving, scrappy environment where the process is still being built.

 - Proficiency with CRM platforms (Salesforce preferred) and sales engagement tools such as Gong, Outreach, Salesloft, or Apollo.

 - Experience selling into insurance, real estate, property management, manufactured housing, or adjacent industries is a plus.
   
   

Why SafeLease?

The tech: Our prospects convert fast because we’re solving real problems and delivering serious value to commercial real estate owners.

The team: We’re a team of seasoned pros and sharp operators who know

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.



This position requires working in the office 3 days per week in either New York City or Austin, TX. Applicants who are unable to work from either office location or are not willing to relocate will not be considered.

 

About The Role

 

We're looking for an Analytics Engineer / Data Analyst to unlock the value of our data assets by transforming and presenting data in a way that drives action. You’ll be a key voice in turning data signals into business decisions.

 

This isn't a "pull reports on request" role. You'll build the infrastructure that makes reliable reporting possible, go deep on unexplained patterns, and proactively surface insights that change how the business operates. You'll work closely with pricing, sales ops, product, and leadership — translating between technical data realities and business questions that don't always arrive in clean form.



What You'll Do

Data Modeling & Transformation

 - Design, build, and maintain dbt models that transform raw source data into clean, well-documented, analytics-ready tables in Snowflake

 - Establish and enforce naming conventions, testing standards, and documentation practices across the dbt project

 - Own the semantic layer — ensuring consistent metric definitions that all stakeholders can trust

Reporting & Dashboards

 - Build and maintain executive and department-level dashboards that communicate performance clearly and without ambiguity

 - Partner with stakeholders across pricing/actuarial, sales, business development, and operations to understand reporting needs and translate them into durable, self-serve solutions

 - Distinguish between dashboards that inform decisions and dashboards that create noise — and build accordingly



Analysis & Insight

 - Conduct deep-dive analyses to explain anomalies, validate hypotheses, and uncover signals in messy data

 - Synthesize findings into clear, concise narratives — written, visual, and verbal — appropriate for technical and non-technical audiences

 - Proactively identify inflection points in the data and connect them to operational or market causes

 - Contribute to strategic decisions by framing tradeoffs with data, not just describing what happened

Data Quality & Governance

 - Instrument data quality checks and alerting so issues surface before they reach decision-makers

 - Maintain data dictionaries and lineage documentation that make the platform legible to the broader organization

 - Partner with engineering to ensure upstream source data lands in a state that's trustworthy and usable

Required

 - 3–5+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data analysis, or a hybrid role

 - Strong SQL — you write queries from scratch, optimize them, and know when a query is te

source_jobs.title: Operations - Summer Intern

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.

That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.



Position Overview

This internship will work closely with our Operations team to support day-to-day business activities, with a primary focus on data entry and record maintenance. This role is ideal for a detail-oriented student who is eager to learn about business operations, gain practical experience working with data systems, and contribute meaningfully to a growing insurtech company. This will be a paid internship that will report into our downtown Austin, Texas office.



Key Responsibilities

 - Accurately enter, update, and maintain data across internal systems, spreadsheets, and databases.

 - Review records for accuracy, completeness, and consistency, flagging discrepancies as needed.

 - Assist the Operations team with organizing, cleaning, and structuring data sets.

 - Support routine administrative and operational tasks as assigned.

 - Help document processes and identify opportunities to improve efficiency.

 - Collaborate with team members across the company to gather and verify information.

 - Maintain confidentiality and adhere to company data-handling standards.

Qualifications

 - Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program, or a recent graduate.

 - Coursework or interest in business, operations, finance, analytics, or related fields.

 - Strong attention to detail and a high level of accuracy.

 - Comfortable working with spreadsheets (Excel or Google Sheets) and basic productivity tools.

 - Curiosity around testing AI in a business setting

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills.

 - Self-motivated, organized, and able to manage time effectively.

 - Reliable, professional, and able to handle sensitive information with discretion.

 - Eagerness to learn and a positive, team-oriented attitude.

What You’ll Gain

 - Hands-on experience supporting a real-world Operations team.

 - Exposure to the day-to-day workings of a growing insurtech company.

 - Mentorship from experienced team members.

 - The opportunity to make a tangible impact during your time with us.

Why SafeLease?

The tech: Our prospects convert fast because we’re solving real problems and delivering serious value to commercial real estate owners.

The team: We’re a team of seasoned pros and sharp operators who know how to move fast and build smart. High standards, low ego.

The stability: We’re well-funded, growing fast, and we make sure our team shares in that success with competitive pay and equity.

The employee experience: We also offer unlimited PTO, full health benefits, flexible work setups, and the kind of culture where people want to show up to do their best work.



If you don't have all the qualifications listed, don't worry! We understand everyone's career path is uniq

We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. THIS POSITION REQUIRES WORKING IN THE OFFICE 3 DAYS PER WEEK IN EITHER NEW YORK CITY OR AUSTIN, TX. APPLICANTS WHO ARE UNABLE TO WORK FROM EITHER OFFICE LOCATION OR ARE NOT WILLING TO RELOCATE WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED.



About The Role

As a Data & Backend Engineer building our Data Platform, you'll design and build our data infrastructure from the ground up: ingestion pipelines that span multiple systems, Python and SQL transformations that give the whole company a trustworthy view of our business, and backend structures making that data accessible where it's needed. You'll be a force multiplier for every team at SafeLease.



What you'll build

End-to-end data pipelines integrating sources across our internal and external apps, 3P vendors and Saas systems

Transformations within and between infrastructure: OLTP, OLAP and S3

Backend Python services and APIs that expose platform data to internal consumers and downstream workflows

Monitoring, alerting, and data quality frameworks so we know when something breaks before anyone else does

Data architecture decisions that will create agility and the ability to scale the business



What we're looking for

3–6 years working on data infrastructure or backend systems in production

Strong Python: you write clean, testable code and know when to reach for a library vs. rolling your own

Strong SQL: complex joins, window functions, CTEs, and query optimization feel natural

Experience designing and shipping cross-system data pipelines (ETL/ELT) that are reliable in the real world

Comfort interpreting data - you become the expert on the data you touch

Collaborative instincts: you'll work closely with product, frontend, analytics, research

Nice to have

Experience with dbt for SQL-native transformation layers

Familiarity with Airflow, Prefect, or another orchestration framework

Exposure to AWS Lambda, IAM, KMS, MWAA, ECS/EKS

Background at an insurtech, proptech, or similarly regulated domain

Opinions about data modeling: star schema, OBT, normalization tradeoffs

Experience being one of the first data engineers at a company



Compensation: $130,000 to $225,000 base, depending on experience and qualifications.

The tech: Our prospects convert fast because we’re solving real problems and delivering serious value to commercial real estate owners.

The team: We’re a team of seasoned pros and sharp operators who know how to move fast and build smart. High standards, low ego.

The employee experience: We also offer unlimited PTO, full health benefits, flexible work setups, and the kind of culture where peopl

source_jobs.title: Claims Adjuster (Nebraska)
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.



This position will be remote but must be based in the state of Nebraska*

Role Overview

We are hiring a versatile Claims Adjuster to join our small-but-mighty claims department. Reporting directly to the Director of Claims, this expanded role is responsible for handling a diverse, non-traditional risks and multi-line insurance portfolio. In addition to managing high volume personal property claims, specifically focusing on the contents of a claimant's storage unit, the successful candidate will evaluate and adjust commercial property and casualty (P&C) claims as well as specialized manufactured housing claims. This is a desk-based adjusting position focusing on streamlined digital processes.

 
 

About the Role

To drive our mission forward, this role will:

 - Lead Claims Resolution: Investigate, evaluate, and adjust a variety of property and casualty claims. This includes high volume personal property contents within storage units, commercial property losses, commercial casualty liabilities, and manufactured housing claims.

 - Drive Fair Settlements: Negotiate effectively and professionally with claimants, public adjusters, legal counsel, and third-party representatives to reach equitable, timely resolutions.

 - Ensure Accuracy: Process payments, issue settlements within designated authority limits, and maintain precise, up-to-date claim records to support operational excellence and strict regulatory compliance.

 - Tackle Complexity: Provide technical expertise and support for high-value, complex, or multi-faceted claims, ensuring thorough investigation and efficient handling.

 - Uphold Standards: Adhere to company policies, guidelines, state insurance regulations, and industry best practices while delivering exceptional customer service.

 - Operate Efficiently: Perform full desk-based adjusting, leveraging modern technology, digital documentation, and remote evaluation tools.

 

About You

The ideal candidate is an adaptable insurance professional who thrives in an agile environment and brings diverse, cross-functional technical expertise.

 - Commercial P&C Expertise: Proven track record in adjusting commercial property losses and commercial casualty/liability lines, including a strong understanding of commercial policy language, business interruption claims, and general liability exposures.

 - Manufactured Housing Experience: Direct experience handling claims related to manufactured housing, modular structures and unique policy endorsements.

 - Personal Property Knowledge: Experience adjusting high-volume personal property claims.

 - Licensing & Qualifications: Active Adjuster License in good standing (or ability to quickly obtain reciprocal licensing). A Bachelor's degree or eq

source_jobs.title: Revenue Lead, Manufactured Housing
source_jobs.description:
About SafeLease

At SafeLease, we're rethinking how P&C insurance is sold in an age of technological change. We believe the industry's biggest inefficiencies aren't technical problems — they're structural ones. And we're building the team to tackle them.




SafeLease is a profitable insurance business that designs, underwrites, and distributes specialty coverage for commercial property owners and their tenants. Most insurance companies either distribute products or bear the risk — we do both. We back our policies with our own capital, which means we control the full stack: product design, tech, and the speed at which we move. That end-to-end ownership lets us offer customers real flexibility, saving time and money for more than 4,000 properties insured for billions in value nationwide.




We're a team of 70, growing over 100% annually, and we've done it without sacrificing profitability or culture. Here, you'll get high discretion and a wide aperture of problems to solve. We embrace the newest technologies, move fast together, and operate with the intensity of a small company where every person's work is visible. If you're looking for a place to sharpen your craft alongside people who take their work seriously, you'll fit right in.




ABOUT THE ROLE

SafeLease is hiring a Revenue Lead to own distribution for our Manufactured Housing vertical — a brand-new business unit built on the playbook that made our self-storage program the category leader. The role owns the commercial engine: how our manufactured housing insurance products reach the market, how partners and customers get served, and how we retain and expand those relationships over time. This is a VP-level seat inside the Manufactured Housing business unit, sitting alongside the General Manager, accountable to the vertical’s revenue number and the health of its customer base. Because the vertical is new, the Revenue Lead also shapes go-to-market strategy from the ground up — not just executing an existing plan.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and execute the go-to-market distribution strategy for SafeLease’s manufactured housing insurance products. B2B2C distribution experience is a meaningful advantage — it shortens ramp.

 - Hire, lead, and coach the sales team selling into manufactured housing. Roll up your sleeves to close deals when needed; think strategically about how the team is structured and how performance is measured.

 - Identify, develop, and own relationships with the distribution partners that matter in this market — community owners and operators, REITs, dealer networks, property management companies, and lending platforms.

 - Construct the distribution mix to balance growth with unit economics. Manufactured housing has distinct buying patterns from self-storage, and the channel mix should reflect that.

 - Bring the customer voice back into the business unit. Inform product improvements, new feature priorities, and underwriting decisions based on what you’re seeing in the market.

 - Own the vertical’s revenue number — quota, retention, and the P&L line — and report results to the General Manager and CEO.





REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 10+ years in revenue leadership, distribution, or commercial roles in insurance, insurtech, or adjacent B2B financial services.

 - Working knowledge of the manufactured housing industry — community operators, consolidation trends, regulatory landscape, resident dynamics. Existing relationships with community operators, REITs, and industry partners that let you hit the ground running are required, not preferred.

 - Track record of launching or scaling a new product line or market entry from a standing start. Not inheriting an established book.

 - Owned a revenue number — quota, retention target, or P&L line — and consistently delivered against it.

 - Hands-on experience building a sales team — recruiting, ramping, performance management, and managing through revenue cycles.

 - Comfort building processes and t

notes: Insurance company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles
